items.title: Here’s how Treasuries could shape Trump’s Iran war and bitcoin moves
items.description: Treasury yields and swap spreads could eventually pressure the Trump administration to moderate the conflict, analysts argue.

items.title: Balancer Labs will shut down as corporate entity became 'a liability' after $110 million exploit
items.description: Co-founder Fernando Martinelli said he considered winding down the entire protocol but decided the team deserved a chance to restructure, with the DAO targeting zero emissions, fee restructuring, and a BAL buyback to offer holders a fair exit.

items.title: Bitcoin, ether, solana prices move higher as Gulf allies inch toward joining Iran war
items.description: Crypto recovered on Tuesday morning even as Monday's relief rally unraveled across traditional markets, with oil jumping 4% on reports that Saudi Arabia and the UAE are moving to join the conflict.

items.title: Aave DAO backs V4 mainnet plan in near-unanimous vote
items.description: Aave founder Stani Kulechov says the proposal will move to a binding onchain vote to formalize V4’s deployment on Ethereum.

items.title: As mass adoption approaches, crypto has forgotten its roots
items.description: Mass adoption risks crypto’s cypherpunk roots. Privacy as a permissionless foundation must reclaim DeFi from surveillance, TradFi and memecoin casinos.

items.title: SEC’s top enforcer clashed over Trump cases before quitting: Report
items.description: The SEC’s handling of cases involving Justin Sun and Elon Musk was among the factors that caused the agency’s top enforcement official to quit, according to sources.

snapshots.source_name: BIS — Press Releases（RSS；若端點變動則以 HTML fallback）

items.title: Statement on the nomination of Hyun Song Shin as Governor of the Bank of Korea
items.description: Following the nomination of Hyun Song Shin as Governor of the Bank of Korea, the BIS today announced that Mr. Shin will step back from his duties with immediate effect. In the interim, the Monetary and Economic Department will be led by Frank Smets, who will serve as Acting Head.

items.title: Ben Gully appointed Secretary General of the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision
items.description: Ben Gully will lead the international standard setter's Secretariat in Basel. Mr Gully has been appointed for a three-year term, starting in August. He is currently Deputy Superintendent at the Office of the Superintendent of Financial Institutions (OSFI) in Canada.

items.title: Hélène Rey appointed as Economic Adviser and Head of Monetary and Economic Department
items.description: Hélène Rey, Professor of Economics at London Business School, appointed as Economic Adviser and Head of the Monetary and Economic Department of the BIS. Ms Rey is appointed for a five-year term, effective September 2026. She will lead the economics work of the Bank and join its Executive Committee.

items.title: Congressional Bill S. 4138 Signed into Law
items.description: On Friday, March 20, 2026, the President signed into law: S. 4138, which waives the 60-day notice requirement for the posthumous honorary promotion of Captain Cody Khork, United States Army. The post Congressional Bill S. 4138 Signed into Law appeared first on The White House.

items.title: Democrats’ DHS Shutdown Enters 35th Day as Airports Plunge into Chaos, Frontline Workers Suffer
items.description: As Radical Left Democrats force the Department of Homeland Security shutdown into its 35th day, everyday Americans are bearing the cost.
